CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-27-2017 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court 3.1385. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-06-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-27-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-24-2013 Continued To May-28-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOSEPH CREITZ, ESQ., 415.269.3675, FOR PLAINTIFF DAVID REPP.
ALLISON B. MOSER, ESQ., AND JENNIFER M. MARTINEZ, ESQ., 650.493.9300, FOR DEFENDANT VARIETY CLUB OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT VARIETY CLUB OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMEN is denied. Motion for summary adjudication is denied on statute of limitations grounds, and as to the first cause of action; the motion is granted as to the second and third causes of action. Regarding the statute of limitations, Defendant shifted the burden. Plaintiff, however, raises the issue of equitable tolling regarding the period while Plaintiff and Defendants considered resolving the matter, including the mediation proposed by Variety. (Repp Decl. 13.) This defeats summary adjudication on statute of limitations grounds. As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff raises a triable issue as to whether there was a valid enforceable oral contract between the parties. Plaintiff testified at his deposition that he "felt that the written contract was more to verify the previously given oral contract which [defendant] had given me." (Repp Depo. 136:8-10). As to the second cause of action for promissory estoppel, defendant shifted the burden and plaintiff failed to present any facts showing that defendant made a clear and unambiguous promise to induce plaintiff's reliance. Plaintiff volunteered to reduce his hours and salary. (Repp. Depo. 43:20 - 44:22; 133:8-11.) As to the third cause of action, summary adjudication is granted. Defendant shifted the burden, and Plaintiff did not dispute any facts. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2013 Continued To Apr-04-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT VARIETY CLUB OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to April 4, 2013 on the Court's own motion. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
